specification.topic-statement: The mail message record stores the fields of a single inter-agent mail item and is the canonical in-memory representation of one message delivered between agents.

scope.in-scope: The mail message data fields and the single constructor that creates and initialises a new in-memory mail message record.
scope.boundary: Persisting mail messages to the database and querying received messages are covered by the mail storage specification.
How the send and wait tools use mail messages to coordinate between agents is covered by the inter-agent messaging specification.

field.name: id
field.note: zero until persisted; assigned by database on insert

field.name: from_uuid
field.note: UUID of the sending agent; deep-copied on construction

field.name: to_uuid
field.note: UUID of the receiving agent; deep-copied on construction

field.name: timestamp
field.note: Unix epoch seconds at construction time

behavior.construction:
A new mail message is created by supplying the sender UUID, recipient UUID, and body text. All three strings are deep-copied into allocations owned by the new message record. The id field is set to zero, the timestamp is set to the current Unix wall-clock time, and the read flag is set to false.
All allocations are children of the caller's talloc context, so freeing the context also frees the message and all its string fields. If any allocation fails, the process aborts.
